conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child - Alex, who's 19 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to understand their current situation to determine eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-levels would typically count as approved education. 

To confirm your eligibility, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex working for 24 hours or more per week, or claiming any benefits in their own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working that much - just focuses on studies really. And no, they don't get any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on the information you've provided, let me make my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old and in approved education (A-levels), which means they qualify under the extended eligibility rules for children under 20 in approved education or training
- You are responsible for Alex as they live with you in the UK
- Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming benefits in their own right
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex's circumstances change (such as finishing education, starting to work 24+ hours per week, or claiming benefits), you'll need to notify HMRC as this could affect your eligibility.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and still in approved education (A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You are responsible for Alex as they live with you in the UK (Manchester). Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming benefits in their own right, which would disqualify them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 19-year-old child Alex who is in approved education (A-levels). Alex is not working excessive hours or claiming benefits independently, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
